name: Cloudflare JWT Missing/Invalid Token Enforcement for Protected API Paths
description: Enforces presence and basic structure validation of Authorization: Bearer <JWT> for sensitive API paths such as /api/private/*, /graphql, and /admin/api/*.
objective: Reduce unauthorized access to sensitive API endpoints by requiring bearer JWTs and rejecting missing or malformed tokens before application processing.
risk_mitigated: Unauthenticated access attempts to protected API routes
Trivial bypass using missing or malformed Authorization headers
Increased attack surface from anonymous probing of sensitive endpoints

rules.description: Match sensitive API paths where Authorization header is missing or does not start with Bearer.
rules.expression: (http.request.uri.path matches "^/api/private(?:/.*)?$" or http.request.uri.path eq "/graphql" or http.request.uri.path matches "^/admin/api(?:/.*)?$") and http.request.method ne "OPTIONS" and (not len(http.request.headers["authorization"][0]) > 0 or not lower(http.request.headers["authorization"][0]) starts_with "bearer ")

rules.description: Match sensitive API paths where bearer token is present but not in basic JWT three-segment structure.
rules.expression: (http.request.uri.path matches "^/api/private(?:/.*)?$" or http.request.uri.path eq "/graphql" or http.request.uri.path matches "^/admin/api(?:/.*)?$") and http.request.method ne "OPTIONS" and lower(http.request.headers["authorization"][0]) starts_with "bearer " and not (http.request.headers["authorization"][0] matches "^[Bb]earer\s+[A-Za-z0-9_-]+\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]+\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$")

deployment_notes: Deploy in log/challenge simulation mode first to baseline legitimate traffic patterns.
Review 7-14 days of events and identify integrations that omit Authorization on protected routes.
Switch action from log to block only after validating no critical client or service impact.
Roll out in stages (non-production, canary, full production) and keep rollback-ready rule versions.
tuning_guidance: Exclude explicitly public API endpoints from protected path patterns before enabling block mode.
Keep CORS preflight (OPTIONS) exempt to avoid breaking browser negotiation.
If GraphQL supports public operations, split endpoint by hostname/path or add allow conditions for known public operations.
For service-to-service auth variants, add explicit exceptions for approved alternative auth mechanisms.
monitoring_guidance: Track counts of rule matches by path, method, user agent, source ASN, and client application identifier.
Alert on sudden spikes in missing/invalid bearer matches that may indicate reconnaissance or auth regressions.
Monitor false positives by correlating blocked requests with application 4xx/5xx and support tickets.
Review top matched endpoints weekly and refine protected path scope and exceptions accordingly.
